query: DynamoDB write throughput scalability 50000 events per second
answer: DynamoDB on-demand capacity mode can scale to handle up to 50,000 write events per second. It automatically adjusts to double the previous peak traffic. For higher throughput, request a quota increase.

results.title: DynamoDB on-demand capacity mode - Amazon DynamoDB
results.snippet: To scale beyond the 40,000 table-level read and write throughput limits for all tables in your account, you can request an increase for this quota. Optionally, you can also configure maximum read or write (or both) throughput per second for individual on-demand tables and global secondary indexes. For more information, see DynamoDB maximum throughput for on-demand tables. For on-demand table quotas, see Read/write throughput. * DynamoDB maximum throughput for on-demand tables. DynamoDB tables using on-demand capacity mode automatically adapt to your application’s traffic volume. On-demand capacity mode instantly accommodates up to double the previous peak traffic on a table. On-demand capacity mode instantly accommodates sustained traffic of up to 100,000 reads per second. If your workload generates more than double your previous peak on a table, DynamoDB automatically allocates more capacity as your traffic volume increases. DynamoDB maximum throughput for on-demand tables. Thanks for letting us know this page needs work.

results.snippet: While DynamoDB can keep scaling to support virtually unlimited throughput, each individual partition is limited to 1,000 write units per second

results.title: DynamoDB maximum throughput for on-demand tables
results.snippet: You can optionally specify maximum read or write (or both) throughput per second on individual tables and associated global secondary indexes (GSIs).
